span.p: Complete Logic Study Journey

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Choose Your Approach

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Foundational Path
Canonical Works & Historical Development

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Practical Path
Textbooks & Study Resources

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Foundation Level - Canon

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Basic Concepts - Canon
Copi & Cohen: Introduction to Logic
Hurley: Concise Introduction to Logic
Beall: Logic - The Basics

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Historical Context - Canon
Kneale & Kneale: Development of Logic
Jorgensen: History of Western Logic
Bobzien: Ancient Logic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Propositional Logic - Canon
Goldrei: Propositional & Predicate Calculus
Kleene: Mathematical Logic
Grassmann: Logic & Discrete Mathematics

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Ancient Logic - Canon
Aristotle: Organon (Complete Works)
Corcoran: Ancient Logic & Modern Interpretations
Sandbach: The Stoics

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Foundation Level - Study

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Basic Logic Textbooks
Bergmann, Moor & Nelson: The Logic Book
Shoenfield: Mathematical Logic
van Dalen: Logic and Structure
Lemmon: Beginning Logic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Introductory Resources
Tomassi: Logic
Smith: An Introduction to Formal Logic
Forbes: Modern Logic
Volpano: Introduction to Mathematical Logic

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Intermediate Level

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Modern Logic Revolution
CANON: Frege: Begriffsschrift
CANON: Russell & Whitehead: Principia Mathematica
STUDY: Dummett: Frege - Philosophy of Language
STUDY: Grattan-Guinness: Search for Mathematical Roots

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Set Theory & Foundations
CANON: Kunen: Set Theory - Independence Proofs
CANON: Halmos: Naive Set Theory
STUDY: Devlin: The Joy of Sets
STUDY: Jech: Set Theory (3rd edition)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Proof Theory
CANON: Takeuti: Proof Theory
CANON: Gentzen: Collected Papers
STUDY: Troelstra & Schwichtenberg: Basic Proof Theory
STUDY: Girard: Proofs and Types

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Mathematical Logic
CANON: Bell & Machover: Course in Mathematical Logic
CANON: Boolos, Burgess & Jeffrey: Computability and Logic
STUDY: Chang & Keisler: Model Theory
STUDY: Marker: Model Theory - An Introduction

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Advanced Level

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Modal Logic
CANON: Chellas: Modal Logic - An Introduction
CANON: Lewis: Counterfactuals
STUDY: Hughes & Cresswell: A New Introduction to Modal Logic
STUDY: Goldblatt: Logics of Time and Computation

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Non-Classical Logics
CANON: Priest: Introduction to Non-Classical Logic
CANON: Troelstra & van Dalen: Constructivism in Mathematics
STUDY: Restall: Introduction to Substructural Logics
STUDY: Dunn & Hardegree: Algebraic Methods

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Philosophical Logic
CANON: Burgess: Philosophical Logic - An Introduction
CANON: Sainsbury: Paradoxes
STUDY: Read: Thinking About Logic
STUDY: Gamut: Logic, Language and Meaning

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Specialized Level

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Computational Logic
CANON: Lloyd: Logic Programming
CANON: Handbook of Automated Reasoning
STUDY: Sterling & Shapiro: The Art of Prolog
STUDY: Fitting: First-Order Logic and Automated Theorem Proving

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Category Theory
CANON: Mac Lane: Category Theory for Working Mathematician
CANON: Lawvere & Schanuel: Conceptual Mathematics
STUDY: Pierce: Basic Category Theory for Computer Scientists
STUDY: Goldblatt: Topoi: The Categorial Analysis of Logic

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Logic Applications
CANON: Huth & Ryan: Logic in Computer Science
CANON: Stenning & van Lambalgen: Human Reasoning
STUDY: Russell & Norvig: Artificial Intelligence (Logic sections)
STUDY: Blackburn & Bos: Representation and Inference

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Contemporary Level

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Quantum Logic
CANON: Beltrametti & Cassinelli: Quantum Logic
STUDY: Nielsen & Chuang: Quantum Computation and Information

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Cutting-Edge Research
CANON: Simpson: Subsystems of Second Order Arithmetic
CANON: Univalent Foundations Program: HoTT Book
STUDY: Kohlenbach: Applied Proof Theory
STUDY: Research Journals & Conference Proceedings

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Practical Study Resources
SOFTWARE: Lean Theorem Prover, Coq, Isabelle/HOL
ONLINE: MIT OpenCourseWare, Stanford Logic courses
EXERCISES: Bergmann: The Logic Book exercises
COMMUNITIES: Logic study groups and forums

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Alternative Entry Points by Background

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Mathematics Background
START: Set Theory canonical texts
SUPPLEMENT: Mathematical logic textbooks

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Philosophy Background
START: Ancient Logic canonical works
SUPPLEMENT: Philosophical logic textbooks

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Computer Science Background
START: Computational logic resources
SUPPLEMENT: Logic programming texts
